VaccineValidateR 0.0.1.0

  • Articles

Evaluate Immunizations Against ACIP Schedule

Source: R/validation.R
validate_immunizations.Rd

This function takes a list of immunizations and evaluates them against minimum intervals based on the ACIP schedule, determining the immunity status across a list of antigens. If any live virus products are included, all live-virus-containing products are evaluated. Either a patient list or dates of birth within a patient list are required. A patient list will not be returned with the immunization data. Either a date the immunization was given or age at immunization in days is required. Using age in days is notably faster for large datasets. Either CVX codes or a map between local immunization antigen data and CVX codes is required. Default column names can be overwritten using the individual parameters. Please use the same column names across files!

Usage

validate_immunizations(
  immunization_data,
  patients,
  lim_cvx_map,
  antigen_to_eval = c("ALL"),
  verbose = TRUE,
  date_of_birth_column_name = "DOB",
  study_id_column_name = "STUDY_ID",
  immunization_code_column_name = "CVX",
  local_immunization_identifier_column_name = "LIM",
  immunization_product_name_column_name = "PRODUCT",
  immunization_date_given_column_name = "DATE_GIVEN",
  age_at_immunization_column_name = "AGE_IMM_GIVEN",
  reference_date = Sys.Date()
)

Arguments

immunization_data

A data.table of immunizations containing at minimum STUDY_ID, PRODUCT, and either DATE_GIVEN or AGE_IMM_GIVEN. If there is no patient table, a DOB column is required. CVX, GIVEN_STATUS, and ADMIN_LOCATION columns are optional.

patients

Optional data.table of patient info, with DOB. Default is NULL, in which case DOB must be present in visit_data.

lim_cvx_map

Optional data.table mapping local immunization identifiers (LIM) to CVX codes. Required if CVX is not present in the immunization data. Must include PRODUCT and CVX. Default is NULL, in which case the system will assume that immunization data has a valid CVX'

verbose

Logical. Default is TRUE. If TRUE, console messages are printed after each step.

immunization_code_column_name

Column name containing CVX codes. Default is "CVX".

local_immunization_identifier_column_name

Column name containing local immunization identifiers. Default is "LIM".

immunization_product_name_column_name

Column name containing product names. Default is "PRODUCT".

immunization_date_given_column_name

Column name containing the date the immunization was given. Default is "DATE_GIVEN".

age_at_immunization_column_name

Column name containing age at immunization in days. Default is "AGE_IMM_GIVEN".

reference_date

Date cutoff for immunizations. Default is Sys.Date().

antigens_to_eval

Character vector of antigens to evaluate. Default is "ALL".

Value

A named list with three data.table elements:

immunizations

Product-level immunization data after processing. Typical columns include:

  • STUDY_ID – patient identifier

  • PRODUCT – vaccine product name

  • CVX – vaccine CVX code (integer)

  • DATE_GIVEN – immunization administration date

  • AGE_IMM_GIVEN – age in days at administration

  • Antigen flags (e.g., POLIO, MMR, HIB) indicating antigen mapping

antigens

Antigen-level dose data including validation checks. Typical columns include:

  • STUDY_ID, ANTIGEN, DOB, PRODUCT, CVX

  • DATE_GIVEN, AGE_IMM_GIVEN

  • DOSE_COUNTER, ABS_ADMIN_COUNTER, TABLE_INDEX

  • Validation fields: VALID, SERIES_COMPLETE, DELAYED, NOTES

  • Interval tracking: INTERVAL, LIVE_INTERVAL, CYCLE, NEXT_DOSE_MIN, NEXT_DOSE_RECOMMENDED

skipped_antigens

Immunizations that were excluded from evaluation due to failing rules (e.g., given before birth, given too early, live vaccine spacing violation). Contains the same structure as antigens plus NOTES indicating the reason.
